contradictions.finding: artifact-tools.md has no explicit result schema — all content passes through side-effects (file writes). Adding alignment_violations to the response requires defining a result schema first.
contradictions.resolution: Define a minimal result schema in the changes section: { status, artifacts_written, alignment_violations: Violation[], next_actions }. This matches the existing Rust implementation's return shape.

contradictions.finding: Merge doesn't have source_dirs context needed for check_with_coverage(). Using check_with_coverage() at merge time would require passing source directories from config.
contradictions.resolution: Use check() (format + logical rules only) at merge time. Coverage analysis is a separate concern run via CLI --coverage flag. This keeps merge fast and avoids config dependency.

assumptions_verified: State transitions do not change — alignment check is a non-blocking side effect, not a new state
alignment_warnings is a passive field in sdd_run_change response — no executor branch change needed
Artifact tools block on format violations (Phase 1 rules) but allow writes with coverage warnings (Phase 2 rules)
Merge appends warnings to implementation.md — does NOT block the merge
